nl.statement: iid observational sampling: observed unit O=(Y,A,X) in R x [0,1] x [0,1]^d, single-observation data law P (a probability Measure on the unit space) with induced marginal P_X, nuisances mu_P, pi_P, p_{X,P}; n-sample law is the i.i.d. product Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin n => P). — Direct architectural analogue: Causalean.Estimation.MinimaxATE.Model (Obs/obsLaw/productLaw/minimaxMiss/nMSE/nMiss_sq_le_nMSE) realizes exactly this i.i.d. two-point world but over a FINITE covariate (X : Fin M, Y : Bool) so all laws are finite PMFs. Here X ranges over [0,1]^d and A over [0,1] with a continuous-in-treatment bump, so the single-observation law is a genuine Measure (Mathlib MeasureTheory + Measure.pi tensorization); cannot reuse the finite-PMF Model verbatim -> define-local over Mathlib, importing the Stat.Minimax Le Cam layer. Search trace: surveyed Causalean/Stat/Minimax/{LeCam,MinimaxRisk,Pinsker,ChiSquared,TotalVariation}, Estimation/MinimaxATE; no continuous-treatment dose-response sampling world present.

potential-outcome causal overlay on S1: the PO process a -> Y(a) on the same probability space, with consistency (Y = Y(A) a.s.) and conditional ignorability (Y(a) indep A | X); the causal target value E_P[Y(t_0)] is identified by the observed-data partial mean theta_P(t_0). — Causalean.PO (POSystem, POSystem.Consistency, counterfactual-independence) is the framework analogue but is a skeleton (sorry-bearing) and graph/regime-indexed, heavier than the scalar continuous-treatment overlay needed here. The construction supplies its own explicit PO realization (draw U ~ Unif[0,1], set Y_zeta(a) via the two-point mean channel), so consistency + ignorability are verified directly on the constructed laws rather than imported. Define-local over Mathlib with PO naming aligned to Causalean.PO. Search trace: surveyed Causalean/PO/{Core,Assumptions}; no continuous-dose PO partial-mean world to reuse verbatim.

regime constants and frontier objects: smoothness/positivity/bound scalars and the evaluation geometry, plus the minimax-risk and published-rate frontier sequences. — Plain real/nat regime parameters (alpha,beta,s,M,c_0,t_0,epsilon_0 : R>0, d,n : N) and two real-valued frontier sequences R_n (minimax MSE, def:minimax-risk) and rho_n (def:published-hoif-rate). No measure-theoretic world; bound directly in Basic.lean.
O_1, ..., O_n are iid draws from P.

review.note: Unfolding IIDSample gives measurable, mutually independent, identically P.dataMeasure-distributed draws; the probability and a.s. A/X support conjuncts realize the declared probability-law and observation-space domains.

standard.name: iid sampling
standard.citation: Tsybakov, A. B. (2009). Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation. Springer.

nl.statement: Y = Y(A) almost surely under P.

review.note: The predicate is exactly the P-a.s. identity O.Y = P.pot O.A O, i.e. Y = Y(A).

standard.name: consistency
standard.citation: Kennedy, E. H., Ma, Z., McHugh, M. D., and Small, D. S. (2017). Nonparametric methods for doubly robust estimation of continuous treatment effects. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, Series B 79(4), 1229-1245.

nl.statement: For every a in [0,1], Y(a) is independent of A conditional on X under P.

review.note: For every a in [0,1], equality of the conditional expectations of every bounded measurable transform of Y(a) given (A,X) and given X is the standard two-way characterization of Y(a) conditionally independent of A given X; measurability and integrability are intrinsic bookkeeping.

standard.name: conditional ignorability for continuous treatments
standard.citation: Kennedy, E. H., Ma, Z., McHugh, M. D., and Small, D. S. (2017). Nonparametric methods for doubly robust estimation of continuous treatment effects. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, Series B 79(4), 1229-1245.

review.note: The predicate is exactly |O.Y| ≤ M P-almost surely.

standard.name: uniform bounded outcome
standard.citation: Bonvini, M., and Kennedy, E. H. (2022). Fast convergence rates for dose-response estimation. arXiv:2207.11825.

nl.statement: [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0] is contained in (0,1).

review.note: Unfolding doseWindow yields Set.Icc (t0 - eps0) (t0 + eps0) ⊆ Set.Ioo 0 1, exactly the stated interval containment.

standard.name: interior evaluation point
standard.citation: Colangelo, K., and Lee, Y.-Y. (2020). Double debiased machine learning nonparametric inference with continuous treatments. arXiv:2004.03036.

nl.statement: pi_P(a|x) >= c_0 for every a in [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0] and every x in [0,1]^d.

review.note: Unfolding doseWindow and cube gives exactly ∀ a ∈ [t₀−ε₀,t₀+ε₀], ∀ x ∈ [0,1]^d, π_P(a|x) ≥ c₀.

standard.name: local positivity
standard.citation: Bonvini, M., and Kennedy, E. H. (2022). Fast convergence rates for dose-response estimation. arXiv:2207.11825.

nl.statement: For every x in [0,1]^d, the function a -> mu_P(a,x) belongs to a Holder ball of order alpha and radius M on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

review.note: Unfolding doseWindow, cube, and HolderBall1D gives exactly uniform order-α, radius-M Hölder membership of a ↦ μ_P(a,x) on the stated window for every x in the cube.

standard.name: Holder smoothness in the treatment coordinate
standard.citation: Tsybakov, A. B. (2009). Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation. Springer.

nl.statement: For every x in [0,1]^d, the function a -> pi_P(a|x) belongs to a Holder ball of order beta and radius M on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

review.note: Unfolding doseWindow, cube, and HolderBall1D gives exactly uniform order-β, radius-M Hölder membership of a ↦ π_P(a|x) on the stated window for every x in the cube.

standard.name: Holder smoothness of the conditional treatment density in the treatment coordinate
standard.citation: Bonvini, M., and Kennedy, E. H. (2022). Fast convergence rates for dose-response estimation. arXiv:2207.11825.

nl.statement: The function x -> mu_P(t_0,x) belongs to a Holder ball of order s and radius M on [0,1]^d.

review.note: Unfolding cube and HolderBallND gives exactly that x ↦ μ_P(t₀,x) belongs to the order-s, radius-M multivariate Hölder ball on [0,1]^d.

standard.name: Holder smoothness in the covariate coordinate
standard.citation: Tsybakov, A. B. (2009). Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation. Springer.

nl.statement: The function x -> pi_P(t_0|x) belongs to a Holder ball of order s and radius M on [0,1]^d.

review.note: Unfolding cube and HolderBallND gives exactly that x ↦ π_P(t₀|x) belongs to the order-s, radius-M multivariate Hölder ball on [0,1]^d.

standard.name: Holder smoothness of the conditional treatment density in the covariate coordinate
standard.citation: Bonvini, M., and Kennedy, E. H. (2022). Fast convergence rates for dose-response estimation. arXiv:2207.11825.

nl.statement: The density p_{X,P} belongs to a Holder ball of order s and radius M on [0,1]^d and is bounded above by M.
nl.frozen_body:
The marginal covariate density \(p_{X,P}\) belongs to the Hölder ball
\[
\mathcal H^s(M;[0,1]^d)
\]
and satisfies
\[
0 \le p_{X,P}(x) \le M
\]
for every \(x\in[0,1]^d\).

review.note: HolderBallND supplies the stated order-s radius-M Hölder condition and the final conjunct the upper bound; explicit nonnegativity is intrinsic to the paper's designation of p_{X,P} as a density.

standard.name: Holder-smooth covariate density
standard.citation: Kennedy, E. H., Balakrishnan, S., Robins, J. M., and Wasserman, L. (2022). Minimax rates for heterogeneous causal effect estimation. arXiv:2203.00837.

nl.statement: There exist a density p_0 on [0,1]^d, a conditional treatment density q_0 on [0,1], and a constant eta_0>0 such that p_0 and q_0 have Holder norms at most M-eta_0 in the smoothness classes required of p_{X,P} and pi_P, q_0(a) >= c_0+eta_0 for all a in [t_0-epsilon_0,t_0+epsilon_0], and p_0 is bounded above by M-eta_0.
nl.frozen_body:
There exist a covariate density \(p_0\) on \([0,1]^d\), a conditional treatment density \(q_0\) on \([0,1]\), a constant \(\eta_0>0\), and a constant \(B_0\) with \(0<B_0<M\), such that \(p_0(x)\ge 0\) for all \(x\in[0,1]^d\), \(q_0(a)\ge 0\) for all \(a\in\mathbb R\),
\[
\int_{[0,1]^d} p_0(x)\,dx=1
\qquad\text{and}\qquad
\int_0^1 q_0(a)\,da=1,
\]
\[
\|p_0\|_{\mathcal H^s([0,1]^d)}\le M-\eta_0,
\]
\(q_0\) has Hölder norm at most \(M-\eta_0\) of order \(\beta\) on \([t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\),
\[
q_0(a)\ge c_0+\eta_0
\qquad\text{for all }a\in[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0],
\]
and
\[
p_0(x)\le M-\eta_0
\qquad\text{for all }x\in[0,1]^d.
\]

review.note: The existential p₀ and q₀ are nonnegative normalized densities with exactly the stated Hölder, upper-bound, and window-positivity slack; the extra B₀∈(0,M) witness is logically redundant because the p₀ density and bound p₀≤M-η₀ on the unit-volume cube force M>η₀>0.

nl.statement: {P : P satisfies ass:iid-sampling, ass:consistency, ass:no-unmeasured-confounding, ass:bounded-outcome, ass:interior-dose, ass:local-positivity, ass:mu-treatment-holder, ass:pi-treatment-holder, ass:mu-covariate-holder, ass:pi-covariate-holder, ass:px-holder}
nl.frozen_body:
With the minimax risk \(R_n(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0)\) and the published HOIF benchmark \(\rho_n\) as in \cref{obj:def:minimax-risk,obj:def:published-hoif-rate}, define
\[
\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)
\]
to be the collection of observed-data laws \(P\) satisfying all of the following conditions:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sampling.)} The observations satisfy \cref{obj:ass:iid-sampling}.
\item \textbf{(Causal identification.)} Consistency and no unmeasured confounding hold as in \cref{obj:ass:consistency,obj:ass:no-unmeasured-confounding}.
\item \textbf{(Outcome boundedness.)} The bounded-outcome condition holds as in \cref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}.
\item \textbf{(Interior dose.)} The evaluation dose \(t_0\) and local radius \(\varepsilon_0\) satisfy \cref{obj:ass:interior-dose}.
\item \textbf{(Local positivity.)} The treatment density obeys the local positivity condition with constant \(c_0\) as in \cref{obj:ass:local-positivity}.
\item \textbf{(Treatment-direction smoothness.)} The maps \(a\mapsto \mu_P(a,x)\) and \(a\mapsto \pi_P(a\mid x)\) satisfy the treatment-direction Hölder conditions in \cref{obj:ass:mu-treatment-holder,obj:ass:pi-treatment-holder}.
\item \textbf{(Covariate-direction smoothness and density range.)} The maps \(x\mapsto\mu_P(t_0,x)\) and \(x\mapsto\pi_P(t_0\mid x)\) satisfy the covariate-direction Hölder conditions in \cref{obj:ass:mu-covariate-holder,obj:ass:pi-covariate-holder}, and \(p_{X,P}\) satisfies the Hölder, nonnegativity, and upper-envelope condition in \cref{obj:ass:px-holder}.
\item \textbf{(Semantic ties to the observed-data law.)} The regression, covariate-density, and treatment-density symbols are tied to \(P\): \(\mu_P(A,X)\) equals the conditional mean of \(Y\) given \((A,X)\) \(P\)-almost surely; \(p_{X,P}\) is the density of the \(X\)-marginal on \([0,1]^d\); and \(\pi_P\) is nonnegative on \([0,1]\times[0,1]^d\) with the \((A,X)\)-law density \((a,x)\mapsto \pi_P(a\mid x)p_{X,P}(x)\) relative to Lebesgue measure on \([0,1]\times[0,1]^d\), as in \cref{obj:ass:mu-is-regression,obj:ass:px-is-x-density,obj:ass:pi-is-cond-treatment-density}.
\end{itemize}

review.note: The eleven listed member assumptions are present exactly; muReg, pxDens, and piCond merely enforce the paper-defined meanings of μ_P, p_{X,P}, and π_P for DoseLaw's free carrier fields, so they do not narrow the paper class.
Unfolding cube gives precisely the Lebesgue integral over [0,1]^d of μ_P(t₀,x)p_{X,P}(x).

nl.statement: R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) = inf_{hat_theta_n} sup_{P in P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0)} E_P[(hat_theta_n - theta_P(t_0))^2]

review.note: The nested infimum and supremum encode the stated n-sample minimax MSE under the product law; restricting to measurable estimators valued in [-M,M] is equivalent by clipping because the class assumptions place θ_P(t₀) in that interval.
After unfolding, Lean's `max` is exactly the paper's `vee` of the same two real-power branches, with identical binders and exponents.

nl.statement: Headline and frontier handle. The delivered content is the all-beta lower floor: by a de-laundered two-point Le Cam construction perturbing only mu_P with the covariate density and treatment-density baselines fixed, the original class satisfies R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)} for every beta>0. When s>=d/4, lem:rho-oracle-regime-algebra identifies the published benchmark rho_n with this same exponent, so the converse lands on the classical interior treatment-regression scale. When 0<s<d/4, rho_n = n^{-2/(1+d/(4*s)+1/alpha)} is recorded only as the published HOIF comparator, not as a same-class certified upper endpoint. The residual carried by oeq:full-beta-frontier is therefore the unrestricted upper frontier itself: can one attain rho_n on the original class, or does a genuinely beta-sensitive phase intervene?
nl.frozen_body:
The beta comparison handle \(\mathfrak B_{\alpha,\beta,s}\) denotes the following frontier statement. For every \(\beta>0\), there is a constant \(c>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\le
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right).
\]
For every \(n\ge 1\), when \(d\le 4s\), the published benchmark satisfies
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}.
\]
For every \(n\ge 1\), when \(0<s\) and \(4s<d\), the published benchmark satisfies
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}
\]
and its exponent obeys
\[
\frac{2}{1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha}
<
\frac{2\alpha}{2\alpha+1}.
\]
The upper-frontier alternatives are recorded as the following disjunction: either there is a constant \(C>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\le
C\rho_n,
\]
or there are \(\kappa\) and \(C>0\) with
\[
\frac{2}{1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha}<\kappa<\frac{2\alpha}{2\alpha+1}
\]
such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\le
C n^{-\kappa},
\]
or there are \(\beta'>0\), \(\beta'\ne\beta\), exponents \(\kappa,\kappa'\) with \(\kappa'\ne\kappa\), and positive constants \(c,C,c',C'\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
c n^{-\kappa}
\le
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\le
C n^{-\kappa}
\]
and
\[
c' n^{-\kappa'}
\le
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta',s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\le
C' n^{-\kappa'}.
\]

review.note: The never-proved, unused handle records under `∀ beta > 0` the eventual all-beta lower floor on the original `HolderDoseClass`, both stated regime identities and the deficient-regime exponent ordering, plus an unresolved disjunction covering rho-rate attainability, an intermediate exponent, or beta-sensitive rates; it certifies no upper endpoint and faithfully records the paper's open frontier.

nl.statement: Assume ass:baseline-submodel-slack. Then for every beta>0 there exists a constant c > 0, depending only on the fixed model radii and the slack baseline, such that for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) >= c n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)). Equivalently, uniformly over every beta>0, every estimator of theta_P(t_0) over P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0) has worst-case mean-squared error at least a constant multiple of the classical interior treatment-regression pointwise rate.

review.note: After unfolding RegimeConstants, BaselineSubmodelSlack, HolderDoseClass, and minimaxRisk, the Lean statement has the paper's ∀ beta>0, ∃ c>0, eventual-in-n lower bound with the identical exponent and model class; the regime premises are standing parameter-domain bookkeeping, and restricting estimators to measurable [-M,M]-valued maps is equivalent by truncation because the class bounds theta_P(t0) in [-M,M].

nl.statement: Under the assumptions of thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound, if s >= d/4 then rho_n = n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)) and therefore R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) >= c n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)) = c rho_n for some c > 0. Thus the certified lower floor reduces to the classical interior pointwise nonparametric regression barrier.

review.note: Unfolding publishedHoifRate, hsd with positive alpha and s makes the oracle branch the max for every n≥1; the eventual conjunction is therefore equivalent to the paper's rate identity plus the sufficiently-large-n minimax lower bound, with the same HolderDoseClass, minimaxRisk, and baseline-slack assumptions.

nl.statement: Under baseline-submodel slack, for every fixed beta > 0 the original dose-response class has minimax MSE at least c n^(-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)) for all sufficiently large n. This lower floor matches the published rho_n exponent when s >= d/4; when 0 < s < d/4, rho_n has a strictly smaller exponent and remains only an upper-side comparator, so no same-class upper endpoint is asserted.

review.note: After unfolding the class, minimax risk, published rate, regime constants, and baseline slack, the Lean statement is equivalent: it gives a beta-dependent positive constant and an eventual oracle lower floor, identifies the published exponent for s ≥ d/4, proves the strictly smaller published exponent for 0 < s < d/4, and asserts no same-class upper endpoint.

nl.statement: Fix B>0. Let Q_u and Q_v be distributions on {-B,B} with means u and v, respectively, so Q_u(B)=(1+u/B)/2 and Q_v(B)=(1+v/B)/2. If |u|<=B/2 and |v|<=B/2, then KL(Q_u,Q_v) <= 2*(u-v)^2/B^2.

review.note: Unfolding twoPointMean gives exactly the {-B,B} laws with masses (1+u/B)/2 and (1-u/B)/2; under B>0 and the central-half bounds, the ENNReal KL inequality is equivalent to the paper's nonnegative real quadratic bound.

nl.statement: Let Q_0 and Q_1 be two laws for the n-sample experiment and let theta_0,theta_1 be the corresponding values of a real parameter, with Delta=|theta_1-theta_0|. If KL(Q_0,Q_1)<=K<infinity, then every estimator T satisfies max_{i=0,1} E_{Q_i}[(T-theta_i)^2] >= c_K Delta^2 for a constant c_K>0 depending only on K.

review.note: The Lean quantifiers make cK positive and uniform over all probability laws, parameter values, and measurable estimators under the KL budget; (theta1-theta0)^2 equals Delta^2, while measurability and squared-loss integrability are legitimate expectation bookkeeping.

nl.statement: If s >= d/4, then the published beta>=alpha rate rho_n satisfies rho_n = n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for every n >= 1.

review.note: Unfolding publishedHoifRate as the maximum of the two published power rates, d≤4s is exactly s≥d/4, and the positivity premises are the intrinsic domain conditions for the smoothness indices and denominators.

nl.statement: Assume ass:baseline-submodel-slack. For every beta>0 there is a constant c_or>0, depending only on the fixed model radii and on the slack baseline but not on n, such that for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c_or n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. The construction leaves the treatment density fixed, so the bound is independent of whether beta>=alpha.

review.note: After unfolding BaselineSubmodelSlack, HolderDoseClass, and minimaxRisk, universal closure over beta with hbeta is the paper's every-beta quantifier, and ∃ cor>0 before the Nat-atTop eventual bound is equivalent to a positive n-independent constant valid for all sufficiently large n.

nl.statement: If 0<s<d/4, then rho_n = n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} for every n>=1, and 2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha) < 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1).

review.note: After unfolding publishedHoifRate as the maximum of the oracle and deficient powers, hs together with 4*s<d is equivalent to 0<s<d/4, and universal closure over n with 1≤n gives exactly the stated identity and strict exponent comparison for every n≥1.

nl.statement: Assume ass:baseline-submodel-slack. For every fixed beta>0 and every s>=d/4, there exists c>0 such that, for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} = c rho_n. Thus, in the smooth-covariate regime, the certified lower floor lands on the same exponent as the published benchmark rho_n, but this core does not claim a matching same-class upper bound.

review.note: After unfolding the model class, minimax risk, benchmark rate, slack condition, and regime constants, `d ≤ 4s` is equivalent to `s ≥ d/4`, `∀ᶠ n atTop` means all sufficiently large n, and the positive witness `c` makes `n^{-2α/(2α+1)} = ρ_n` equivalent to the paper's scaled equality `c n^{-2α/(2α+1)} = cρ_n`; the additional positivity/interiority premises are global setup conditions.

nl.statement: For every fixed beta>0 and every 0<s<d/4, under the declared same-class assumptions and ass:baseline-submodel-slack, there exists c>0 such that, for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. Moreover rho_n = n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} has a strictly smaller exponent than the certified lower floor. The same-class upper endpoint C rho_n is not discharged on the present core.

review.note: After unfolding RegimeConstants, BaselineSubmodelSlack, HolderDoseClass, minimaxRisk, thetaFunctional, and publishedHoifRate, the hypotheses encode beta>0, 0<s<d/4, the standing same-class conditions, and strict baseline slack, while the conclusion gives exactly an eventual positive-constant lower floor and the deficient-rate identity with strictly smaller exponent; the absent upper endpoint is correctly left unasserted.

nl.statement: Assume ass:baseline-submodel-slack. For every fixed beta>0 there exists c>0 such that, for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. If s>=d/4 then rho_n = n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. If 0<s<d/4 then rho_n = n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} and 2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha) < 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1). Hence the certified unrestricted beta-frontier is the all-beta oracle lower floor together with the algebraic comparison to the published rho_n benchmark.

review.note: The existential positive constant and Nat-atTop clause exactly encode the eventual all-beta oracle floor, while the separate ∀ n≥1 conditional conjuncts exactly encode both published-rate regimes, including equality at s=d/4 and the strict deficient exponent comparison.

nl.statement: Cited conditional comparator (Bonvini-Kennedy 2022). Under Assumptions 1-2 and Conditions 1-4 of the source theorem, alpha <= beta, equal covariate smoothness s for the two nuisance functions, projection approximation of order k^(-2s/d), the source's optimal choices of k and h, equal-rate estimation of w and 1/pi, and negligibility of the third-order density-estimation remainder ||v||_g ||q||_g ||p_hat(t,.)-p(t,.)||_infinity, the conditional MSE of the source's specified second-order estimator is eventually at most a constant times rho_n as displayed in Figure 1. This is an estimator-specific conditional risk implication, not an unconditional minimax upper bound over any class and not a same-class upper theorem for def:holder-dose-class.
nl.frozen_body:
For covariate dimension \(d\) and parameters \(\alpha,\beta,s,\gamma_1,\gamma_2\), consider any Bonvini--Kennedy HOIF estimator specification \(E\) consisting of an observed-data law \(P\), evaluation dose \(t_0\), nuisance estimators \(\hat\mu_n,\hat\pi_n,\hat p_n\), kernel \(K\), bandwidths \(h_n\), projection dimensions \(k_n\), and true and estimated projection kernels \(\Pi_n\) and \(\widehat\Pi_n\). Suppose the following conditions of \citet[Theorem~3.1 and \S3.4]{bonvini2022} hold for these same inputs: \(P\) is a probability law; \(\mu_P\), \(p_{X,P}\), and \(\pi_P\) are respectively the outcome regression, covariate density, and conditional treatment density of \(P\); there exist \(0<\ell\le u\) such that, for every \(n,a,x\), \(\ell\le\pi_P(a\mid x)\le u\) and \(\ell\le\hat\pi_n(a\mid x)\le u\); there exists \(B>0\) such that \(|Y|\le B\) and \(|A|\le B\) \(P\)-almost surely and \(|\hat\mu_n(a,x)|\le B\) for every \(n,a,x\); and, for every \(n\) and \(x\), the maps \(a\mapsto\mu_P(a,x)\) and \(a\mapsto\hat\mu_n(a,x)\) lie in the one-dimensional Hölder ball of order \(\alpha\) and radius \(1\) on \(\mathbb R\), while \(a\mapsto\pi_P(a\mid x)\) and \(a\mapsto\hat\pi_n(a\mid x)\) lie in the corresponding ball of order \(\beta\). Writing \(K_{h_n,t_0}(a)=h_n^{-1}K((a-t_0)/h_n)\), assume also that there exist \(K_{\max}>0\) and \(0<g_{\ell}\le g_u\) such that \(|K(u)|\le K_{\max}\), \(\operatorname{supp}(K)\subseteq[-1,1]\), \(\int K(u)\,du=1\), \(\int u^jK(u)\,du=0\) for \(1\le j\le\lceil\alpha\rceil-1\), \(h_n>0\), \(\int K_{h_n,t_0}(a)\,da=1\), and, for every \(n,x\), \(g_{\ell}\le\int K_{h_n,t_0}(a)\pi_P(a\mid x)p_{X,P}(x)\,da\le g_u\); that there exists \(C_\Pi>0\) with \(\Pi_n(x,x)\le C_\Pi k_n\) and \(\widehat\Pi_n(x,x)\le C_\Pi k_n\) for every \(n,x\); and that there exist \(0<r_{\ell}\le r_u\) such that, for every \(n,x\),
\[
r_{\ell}\le
\frac{\int K_{h_n,t_0}(a)\pi_P(a\mid x)p_{X,P}(x)\,da}
{\int K_{h_n,t_0}(a)\hat p_n(a,x)\,da}
\le r_u .
\]
Assume further that \(d>0\), \(\alpha>0\), \(\beta>0\), \(s>0\), \(\alpha\le\beta\), \(\gamma_1=\gamma_2=s\), and that there exists \(B_X>0\) such that, for every \(a\) and \(n\), the maps \(x\mapsto\mu_P(a,x)\), \(x\mapsto\hat\mu_n(a,x)\), \(x\mapsto\pi_P(a\mid x)\), and \(x\mapsto\hat\pi_n(a\mid x)\) lie in the \(s\)-Hölder ball of radius \(B_X\) on \([0,1]^d\). Let \(v_n(x)=\hat\mu_n(t_0,x)-\mu_P(t_0,x)\) and \(q_n(x)=\hat\pi_n(t_0\mid x)^{-1}-\pi_P(t_0\mid x)^{-1}\). The rate specialization requires
\[
\|v_n-\Pi_n v_n\|_{L^2([0,1]^d)}
\|q_n-\Pi_n q_n\|_{L^2([0,1]^d)}
\lesssim k_n^{-(\gamma_1+\gamma_2)/d}
\]
eventually, \(\|v_n\|_{L^2([0,1]^d)}\asymp\|q_n\|_{L^2([0,1]^d)}\) eventually, and
\[
\bigl(\|v_n\|_{L^2([0,1]^d)}
\|q_n\|_{L^2([0,1]^d)}
\|\hat p_n(t_0,\cdot)-\pi_P(t_0\mid\cdot)p_{X,P}(\cdot)\|_{\infty,[0,1]^d}\bigr)^2
\lesssim \rho_n
\]
eventually. The tuning satisfies, when \(d\le 4s\), \(h_n\asymp n^{-1/(2\alpha+1)}\) and \(k_n\asymp nh_n\) eventually, and, when \(4s<d\), \(h_n\asymp n^{-4s/(\alpha(4s+d)+4s)}\) and \(k_n\asymp(nh_n)^{2d/(d+4s)}\) eventually. Then the specified Bonvini--Kennedy HOIF estimator \(\hat\theta_n\), with its nuisance-training outputs held fixed, attains the published benchmark conditional mean-squared-error rate of \cref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate}: there exists \(C>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
\int (\hat\theta_n(O_1,\ldots,O_n)-\theta_P(t_0))^2\,dP^n
\le C\rho_n,\qquad
\rho_n=
\max\left\{n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)},
n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}\right\}.
\]

review.note: The cited predicate self-containedly quantifies over the explicitly specified second-order HOIF estimator, encodes the source theorem conditions and Figure-1 specialization on its actual nuisance errors and tuning sequences, and concludes the correct eventual conditional-MSE upper bound by a positive constant times rho_n without asserting a minimax or HolderDoseClass upper bound.
